name: Marda Blackwater
description: Broad-shouldered leader of The Dredgers, risen from the dredge-boats during a labor dispute five years ago. A powerful working-class champion (Brawn 75, Oratory 78, Influence 75) who rallies dock labor against the merchant princes and -- above all -- against the Baron's Dragon Knights.
narrative: Marda Blackwater leads The Dredgers, Purewater's organization of dock workers and canal laborers. A broad-shouldered woman in her forties, she began as a dredger herself and rose to prominence during a bitter labor dispute five years ago; she is practical, passionate, and utterly committed to improving conditions for the city's working class. She is no trained soldier but a formidable physical presence and a galvanizing speaker (Oratory 78), able to turn out the docks at a word. She'll work with the progressive wing of the Order of the Lake Lady and with ethical merchants, keeps a wary distance from Crowbill, and reserves real hatred for the merchant princes, the Governor, and the Baron's Dragon Knights -- to her, the purest face of aristocratic oppression.

extras.role: Leader of The Dredgers -- broad-shouldered woman in her forties who started as a dredger and rose during a labor dispute five years ago. Practical, passionate, committed to the working class.
extras.faction: The Dredgers -- cautiously allied with progressive elements of the Order of the Lake Lady and ethical merchants; complicated relationship with Crowbill; hostile to the merchant princes, the Governor, and especially the Baron and his Dragon Knights as the ultimate aristocratic oppressors.
extras.source: GM-authored from source lore (purewater-organizations.md, The Dredgers)
